target.name: Blackshark.ai

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $55M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Blackshark.ai is a Series A defense and security technology company that has raised $55M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage band, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, with valuations ranging from $125M to $3.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $55M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying a valuation of $110M. The peer-median method surveys the 8 same-sector, adjacent-stage private companies and derives a median valuation of $1.0B, establishing an upper anchor. These methods yield a low implied value of $94M and a high of $1.1B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $94M to $1.1B, with a median of $555M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around execution risk, market timing of the Defense & Security venture cohort, and degree of product-market fit relative to more mature peer benchmarks. The low end anchors conservatively to capital raised plus minimal markup; the high end reflects peer-set valuations but assumes Blackshark.ai reaches comparable scale and revenue traction.
